name: North Dallas Bank & Trust Co.
description_short: Community bank serving North Texas since 1961, offering personal banking, business lending, and wealth management with 37 consecutive years of 5-star BauerFinancial ratings.
description_long:
North Dallas Bank & Trust Co. (NDBT) was founded in 1961 as a community bank with $2 million in assets and has grown into a regional Texas institution with locations across the Dallas-Fort Worth metroplex. The bank was established by founding directors Bert Fields, Porter Lindsley Sr., and W.F. English, who purchased land at the corner of LBJ Freeway and Preston Road, which became the 12-story North Dallas Bank Tower. Over six decades, NDBT has expanded to Plano, Las Colinas, Frisco, and Addison, maintaining its focus on relationship-based banking and community service.

NDBT offers a comprehensive suite of banking services across three primary segments: personal banking with checking and savings accounts, mortgages, and consumer loans; commercial banking with business accounts and streamlined lending processes; and trust and wealth management services. Their personal banking options include traditional checking accounts, senior-focused accounts, savings accounts, CDs with competitive rates, and mortgage products. The bank also provides digital banking tools and financial calculators for customers.

What distinguishes NDBT is its consistent recognition by BauerFinancial, holding the distinction of being the only Texas bank to earn 5-star ratings for 37 consecutive years as of 2026. The bank partners with the IntraFi Network to provide enhanced FDIC deposit protection beyond standard limits. NDBT emphasizes personal relationships and community investment while adopting big bank technology and security standards. Their recent rebranding in 2021 and ongoing renovations, including the Fields Legacy Banking Center in Frisco, reflect commitment to modernization.

As a regional community bank, NDBT serves customers who prioritize local relationships and stability over national scale. However, the bank's reach is geographically limited to the Dallas-Fort Worth area, and as a community bank, it may have fewer digital convenience features compared to major national banks or fintech alternatives. The focus on personal service suggests longer transaction times than fully automated competitors.
services: Savings accounts and CDs with competitive interest rates
Digital banking with online account management
Financial calculators for loans and personal planning
ATM access across multiple banking centers
IntraFi Network enhanced FDIC deposit protection
Security guidance and identity protection information
pros: Only Texas bank with 37 consecutive years of 5-star BauerFinancial ratings (as of 2026)
Partners with IntraFi Network for enhanced FDIC deposit protection beyond standard insurance limits
Offers personalized service across personal banking, commercial lending, and wealth management
Over 60 years of customer loyalty with deep community roots in North Texas
Combines modern technology and security with community bank relationship focus
Multiple banking centers across Dallas-Fort Worth metro (Addison, Dallas, Frisco, Las Colinas, Plano)
Provides diverse personal banking options including senior accounts and fresh-start debit cards
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to Dallas-Fort Worth area only
As a community bank, likely fewer digital convenience features than major national banks or neobanks
No mention of competitive rates or promotional offers compared to larger national competitors
Smaller institution may have more limited business lending capacity for larger commercial clients
Website lacks specific details on current rates, fees, or APR information for products
best_for: North Texas residents and business owners who prioritize relationship-based banking and local community involvement
Customers seeking long-term banking stability with demonstrated financial strength and regulatory recognition
Business owners needing personalized commercial lending and streamlined account management in the DFW area
Individuals interested in wealth management and trust services with personal advisor relationships
diagnosis: NDBT is best for North Texas residents and business owners who value personal relationships, community connection, and demonstrated financial stability over maximum digital convenience or national scale. The primary caveat is geographic limitation—the bank operates only in the Dallas-Fort Worth area, making it unavailable for customers outside this region, and its community-bank model may offer fewer automated features than large national or digital-only competitors.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
